And both of those are effectively a problem when we don\'t as a society cultivate agency in folks as much as we should or as well as we should. Especially in a day and age where like with something like jobs, you gotta be high agency as. Right. Like this is so hard to be applying to hundreds of places a week to have the discipline to learn a skill. Because that\'s what it is. Job hunting is a skill.

11:54**\

From a purely pedagogical perspective, no way about how we job search is going to make us better job seekers. Which was sort of the. The realization for me. We went from a coaching app that was doing a pretty damn good job. And I would encourage you to use careerspan. Actually, the big use case for someone like yourself would actually be just to use Career Span\'s conversational coaching to record your experiences. I mean, frankly, it\'s been a minute. You\'ve presumably had a little bit of time where you\'re like, I\'m going to focus on parenthood and not think about it.

**\
12:35**\
Vrijen Attawar\
And now you\'re getting back into it, so you\'ll be rusty and you won\'t remember as much about your achievements as you should. So from a purely career coaching perspective, regardless of whether you use Career Span or not, that would be one of the things that I really do. And it\'s doubly important for someone that is aiming to make a shift in a as yet undefined direction, because then what you want to do is a inventory, like a stock take of what you bring to the table. Because that is a great starting point from which to. Instead of the big broad, I could do anything conversation, it makes you go to the more productive. What are the most achievable things, given the stock take of capabilities that I have?

I think, very much candidly this year was about going from a decent concept to a decent product. Just to show you why I felt compelled to show you the product again. 16:39**\

The goal of Careerspan is to get you to the top of the stack. We are increasingly getting jobs distributed to Careerspan through Careerspan. We do a limited number of jobs that are released every week. And so if you\'re one of the target role types, you might get a recommendation or something will show up in your inbox. But the. And that\'s part of our monetization strategy is Getting jobs in. But from a candidate perspective, I think what may be a lot more concrete and useful to you now is not just the stories for the sake of sharing them, but also the actual vibe checks. Because what you get out of these is you paste in a job, you get a analysis, but more importantly, you get to see for each job type what skills you\'re missing. Like, what is.

**\
17:25**\
Vrijen Attawar\
So, for example, this is totally missing. It\'s not even on your resume, but this is on your resume. But we think you should have, like, more evidence in the form of a story. So that\'s one value add for folks that want a more systematic way of thinking. Like, okay, am I missing something in how I\'m presenting myself to a particular employer? The other value add is this section where you get a lot of really solid language that you can use in your outreach. It\'s essentially a, like, how would I sell you for this role if I could sell you. Right, what would your strategic positioning be? And, you know, regardless of your school, it\'ll. It\'ll. It\'ll make a. It\'ll make an effort. Right. It\'ll try to. A resume is a very narrow slice of who someone is, and you can only say so many specific, unique things about someone without devolving into generic nonsense. Right. If you\'re just working off of a resume, that moment that you identified, that\'s exactly what we\'re hoping to drive. It\'s that exact moment of like, huh, I guess I never asked myself that question.

Which is also where the. As I\'m sure that you\'re a very proficient user of PLOD projects, but the value is in the externalization of the inquiry because you\'re no longer leading the inquiry. So you are being pushed by the AI. And that\'s sort of. We\'ve optimized for. Is to push your thinking into uncomfortable directions that yourself may not go in. But that\'s where the stuff is. You know, it\'s. It\'s the parts that we are so fucking cheesy, but it\'s like the parts that we\'re the most insecure about is actually where the substance lies in at least in a lot of.

I appreciate it. You\'re, you said you\'re building like you said you\'re trying to replace their job board. Are you building like a ghetto? Like.

**\
25:21**\
Vrijen Attawar\
So right now, candidly, right now we\'ve built a consumer tool that works really well, but we don\'t in our heart of hearts want to charge job seekers. So we\'re trying to figure out how do we use this tech. That\'s awesome, but in a way that\'s more aligned with our values. I think where we\'ve landed is essentially being able to distribute jobs through Career Span so effectively. You know, you as a hiring manager, do you want to sit on hr, you know, wait on hr, go through all the effort? Especially in an early stage company you probably don\'t even have hr. You instead hand out. Yeah, exactly. You hand out Career Span and you apply through using Career Spanish. So you\'ll get scanned automatically the moment you log in, upload your resume and click, you know, I\'m interested.

**\
26:08**\
Vrijen Attawar\
But then as you add stories, we will actually reevaluate you each time you add a story. If you get to the threshold, then we\'ll put you forward. So it\'s meant to be a more pro candidate way of prepping yourself with the upside that you then never have to redo anything for Pure Span company. Right. Your profile is always ready. You just click apply and you\'re good.

**\

Anthony DeLuise\
And business side, you get like, you know, fraud protection and all this stuff to make sure you\'re like a real person eventually and. Yeah, yeah, exactly.

**\

I mean, you certainly can\'t auto apply through Career Span, you know, so there\'s that.
